title: SelectionCommandInput Object
description: Provides a command input to get a selection from the user.

methods.name: addSelection
methods.description: Adds the selection to the list of selections associated with this input. This method is not valid within the commandCreated event but must be used later in the command lifetime. If you want to pre-populate the selection when the command is starting, you can use this method in the activate method of the Command. It's also valid to use in other events once the command is running, such as the validateInputs event.

methods.name: addSelectionFilter
methods.description: Adds an additional filter to the existing filter list.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: clearSelection
methods.description: Clears the current selection so no entities are in the selection.

methods.name: clearSelectionFilter
methods.description: Clears the list of selection filters.

methods.name: getSelectionLimits
methods.description: Get the limits currently defined for this input.

methods.name: selection
methods.description: Returns the selection at the specified index.

methods.name: setSelectionLimits
methods.description: Defines the limits for the number of selections associated with this input. A maximum value of 0 indicates that there is no maximum.

properties.name: commandInputs
properties.description: Gets the CommandInputs class of the parent, which can be a Command, GroupCommandInput or TabCommandInput.

properties.name: commandPrompt
properties.description: Gets or sets the tooltip shown next to the cursor.

properties.name: hasFocus
properties.description: Gets and sets if this selection input has focus with respect to other selection inputs on the command dialog. Only one selection input on a dialog can have focus at a time, so setting hasFocus to true will remove the focus from the selection input that previously had focus. When a selection input has focus; any user selections will be added to that selection input, and the selection rules associated with that selection input will apply.   Setting hasFocus to True for a selection input whose isVisible property is false will fail.

properties.name: id
properties.description: Gets the unique identifier for this input in the command's CommandInputs.

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.description: Gets or sets if this input is currently enabled or disabled for user interaction.   Currently, the isEnabled property does not disable SelectionCommandInput objects but instead has the same effect as the SelectionCommandInput.hasFocus property.

properties.name: isFullWidth
properties.description: Gets or sets if this input fills the entire width of the dialog. If true, the name is ignored and the input control will fill the entire width of the command dialog. The default value for this property in a new command input if false, or not to fill the width. This property does not apply to GroupCommandInputs or TabCommandInputs.

properties.name: isUseCurrentSelections
properties.description: Determines if any selections the user has made before starting the command can be used by the command's selection inputs. The default is true, which means the active selections will be added to the first selection input whose selection filter allows for that entity type. For example, if you have two selection inputs that have filters to select any number of faces and there are four faces selected when the command is started, those four faces will be selected by the selection input. If there's another selection input for the same command that has the filter set to select sketch curves, and there are faces and sketch curves selected when you start the command, the faces will be selected by the selection input filtering for faces, and the sketch curves will be selected by the selection input filtering for sketch curves.   You can programmatically control which selected entities will be added to the selection inputs by using the preSelect event of the command. The preSelect event will fire for each entity that was already selected before it's added to the selection input, and you can use it to control if it will be added to the selection input.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: isVisible
properties.description: Gets or sets if this input will be visible to the user.   Setting a SelectionCommandInput to be invisible will clear any selections it currently has.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Gets the user visible name of this input.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: parentCommandInput
properties.description: Gets the parent CommandInput if this commandInput is the child of a TabCommandInput or GroupCommandInput. Returns null if there is no parent.

properties.name: selectionCount
properties.description: Gets the current number of selections the user has made for this input.

properties.name: selectionFilters
properties.description: Gets or sets the list of selection filters.

properties.name: toolClipFilename
properties.description: Gets or sets the full filename of the image file (PNG) used for the tool clip. The tooltip is always shown but as the user hovers over the control it will progressively display the tool clip and description text.

properties.name: tooltip
properties.description: Gets or sets the base tooltip string. This is always shown for commands. If the tooltip description and/or tool clip are also specified then the tooltip will progressively display more information as the user hovers the mouse over the control.

properties.name: tooltipDescription
properties.description: Gets or sets additional text to display progressively along with the tooltip. The text for the description can contain some basic HTML formatting tags to format the tags. For example the br tag can be used to create multiple paragraphs. The tooltip is always shown but as the user hovers over the control it will progressively display the tool clip and description text.

related_samples.name: Command Inputs API Sample
related_samples.description:
Creates a command dialog that demonstrates all of the available command inputs.

To use the sample, create a new Python or C++ script and copy and paste this code, replacing the default code.  You also need to unpack this zip file which contains a resource folder into the same folder where the source code file (.py or .cpp) is.

addSelection.title: SelectionCommandInput.addSelection Method
addSelection.description: Adds the selection to the list of selections associated with this input. This method is not valid within the commandCreated event but must be used later in the command lifetime. If you want to pre-populate the selection when the command is starting, you can use this method in the activate method of the Command. It's also valid to use in other events once the command is running, such as the validateInputs event.

syntax.python: returnValue = selectionCommandInput_var.addSelection(selection)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = selectionCommandInput_var->addSelection(selection);

addSelection.return_description: Returns true if a selection to the entity was added to this input.

parameters.name: selection
parameters.description: The entity to add a selection of to this input. The addition may fail if the entity does not match the selection filter, or adding it would exceed the limits.

addSelectionFilter.title: SelectionCommandInput.addSelectionFilter Method
addSelectionFilter.description: Adds an additional filter to the existing filter list.

syntax.python: returnValue = selectionCommandInput_var.addSelectionFilter(filter)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = selectionCommandInput_var->addSelectionFilter(filter);

addSelectionFilter.return_description: Returns true if the filter was added successfully.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description: The name of a selection filter to add. The valid list of selection filters can be found here: Selection Filters.

classType.title: SelectionCommandInput.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

syntax.python: returnValue = adsk.core.SelectionCommandInput.classType()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = adsk::core::SelectionCommandInput::classType();

classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

clearSelection.title: SelectionCommandInput.clearSelection Method
clearSelection.description: Clears the current selection so no entities are in the selection.

syntax.python: returnValue = selectionCommandInput_var.clearSelection()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = selectionCommandInput_var->clearSelection();

clearSelectionFilter.title: SelectionCommandInput.clearSelectionFilter Method
clearSelectionFilter.description: Clears the list of selection filters.

syntax.python: returnValue = selectionCommandInput_var.clearSelectionFilter()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = selectionCommandInput_var->clearSelectionFilter();

deleteMe.title: SelectionCommandInput.deleteMe Method

syntax.python: returnValue = selectionCommandInput_var.deleteMe()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = selectionCommandInput_var->deleteMe();

deleteMe.return_description: Returns true if the delete was successful.

getSelectionLimits.title: SelectionCommandInput.getSelectionLimits Method
getSelectionLimits.description: Get the limits currently defined for this input.

syntax.python: (returnValue, minimum, maximum) = selectionCommandInput_var.getSelectionLimits()
syntax.cpp: #include getSelectionLimits(minimum, maximum);

getSelectionLimits.return_description: Returns true if the selection limits were successfully returned.

parameters.name: minimum
parameters.description: The minimum number of selections required. A value of zero means that there is no minimum limit.

parameters.name: maximum
parameters.description: The maximum number of selections required. A value of zero means that there is no maximum limit.

selection.title: SelectionCommandInput.selection Method
selection.description: Returns the selection at the specified index.

syntax.python: returnValue = selectionCommandInput_var.selection(index)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = selectionCommandInput_var->selection(index);

selection.return_description: Returns the Selection at the specified index, or null on error.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the selection to return.

setSelectionLimits.title: SelectionCommandInput.setSelectionLimits Method
setSelectionLimits.description: Defines the limits for the number of selections associated with this input. A maximum value of 0 indicates that there is no maximum.

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = selectionCommandInput_var.setSelectionLimits(minimum)

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = selectionCommandInput_var.setSelectionLimits(minimum, maximum)

setSelectionLimits.return_description: Returns true if the limits were successfully set.

parameters.name: minimum
parameters.description: The minimum number of selections required. A value of zero means that there is no minimum limit.

parameters.name: maximum
parameters.description: The maximum number of selections required. A value of zero means that there is no maximum limit. If maximum is equal to minimum, then exactly that number of selections is required.   This is an optional argument whose default value is 0.
